channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump sues Des Moines Register, top pollster for 'brazen election interference,' fraud over Harris poll
item.description: FIRST ON FOX: President-elect Donald Trump is suing the Des Moines Register and its top pollster Ann Selzer for “brazen election interference" and fraud over its final 2024 presidential poll showing Vice President Kamala Harris leading him in Iowa, despite his ultimate victory in the state by m
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jill Biden says she has closed the book on teaching at Northern Virginia Community College
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Jill Biden has closed the book on her teaching career at a Virginia community college. The first lady, who has spent the past 40 years teaching in classrooms, announced Monday that she had taught her final class at Northern Virginia Community College in Alexandria last week.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Judge rejects Trump’s bid to toss hush money conviction
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge Monday refused to throw out President-elect Donald Trump’s hush money conviction because of the U.S. Supreme Court's recent ruling on presidential immunity. But the overall future of the historic case remains unclear. Manhattan Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Judge rejects Trump’s bid to toss hush money conviction because of Supreme Court immunity ruling
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — A judge Monday refused to throw out President-elect Donald Trump’s hush money conviction because of the U.S. Supreme Court's recent ruling on presidential immunity. But the overall future of the historic case remains unclear. Manhattan Judge Juan M.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL R. SISAK, JAKE OFFENHARTZ and JENNIFER PELTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Yet another attempt to pass Political Flag Ban at the Statehouse
item.description: If one state senator can shore up enough support in Tallahassee, political flags will no longer be allowed to be flown in public buildings.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump says it was 'stupid' for Biden to let Ukraine use US weapons to strike deeper into Russia
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump says he might reverse President Joe Biden’s decision to permit Ukrainian forces to use American long-range weapons to strike deeper into Russian territory.
item.dc:creator: Colleen Long And Aamer Madhani, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Senate advances NDAA, teeing up final passage for annual defense policy bill
item.description: The Senate voted to advance its annual $895 billion defense policy bill, a signal that the legislation is on track to pass despite Democratic grumblings over a transgender care provision.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Huffman wins key vote to succeed Grijalva atop Natural Resources Democrats
item.description: Rep. Jared Huffman (D-Calif.) won a Democratic Steering and Policy Committee vote Monday to succeed Rep. Raul Grijalva (D-Ariz.) as the top Democrat on the House Natural Resources Committee, advancing him to a full vote of the Democratic caucus.
item.dc:creator: Zack Budryk, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bill to officially make bald eagle national bird heads to Biden’s desk
item.description: A bill to officially designate the bald eagle as the national bird will head to President Biden’s desk after passage in the House on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Two charged in connection with Iran-backed drone strike that killed 3 US troops in the Middle East
item.description: Two men, including a dual Iranian American citizen, have been arrested on charges that they exported sensitive technology to Iran that was used in a drone attack in Jordan that killed three American troops early this year and injured dozens of other servicemembers.
item.dc:creator: Steve Leblanc, Eric Tucker And Tara Copp, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Former FBI informant pleads guilty to fabricating Biden claims
item.description: A former FBI informant on Monday entered a guilty plea on four federal charges related to falsifying statements that in part spurred a GOP congressional investigation into the Biden family.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, Fox 5

item.title: Top Republican demands answers from Zuckerberg, accusing Meta of ‘shadow banning’ military content
item.description: Sen. Joni Ernst, R-Iowa, said in a letter to Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg that he needs to answer for purported "shadow banning" of military-related postings.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Supreme Court declines appeal from Peter Navarro; Trump White House aide to return after prison term
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court turned back an appeal Monday from Peter Navarro, the former White House official who is set to return in Donald Trump's second term after serving prison time on contempt of Congress charges.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch: Biden speaks at Labor Department
item.description: President Biden spoke early Monday afternoon at the Labor Department to highlight his administration's efforts to strengthen America's workforce.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Capitol Hill braces for high-stakes showdown over $36T US debt crisis
item.description: A new economic projection suggests Congress could be headed for a showdown over the U.S. debt limit by mid-June.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former FBI informant will plead guilty to lying about phony bribery scheme involving the Bidens
item.description: A former FBI informant is set to plead guilty to lying about a phony bribery scheme involving President Joe Biden and his son, Hunter, that became central to a Republican impeachment inquiry in Congress.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Youth movement among House Democrats is overrated
item.description: The youth movement among House Democrats? Don't believe the hype. Even as a few Democratic committee heads are being pushed aside for younger replacements, the party is elevating some of its most senior members to lead virtually every major committee in the next Congress.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump claims a 'massive' mandate, but presidents often overread their victories
item.description: There's a long history of presidents claiming that their win means the will of the people is behind their agendas. But elections are rarely full-throated endorsements of a candidate's every policy.
item.dc:creator: Domenico Montanaro, MPR News

item.title: Trial over Minnesota House race that could swing control of chamber focuses on lost ballots
item.description: Republicans challenged the election result arguing that a 14-vote margin between the DFL representative who won and his GOP opponent could be made up in a pool of 20 ballots that were accidentally tossed.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Journalists anticipate a renewed hostility toward their work under the incoming Trump administration
item.description: When Donald Trump takes office as president for a second time, many journalists anticipate a hostile climate toward them and their work.
item.dc:creator: David Bauder, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Lawmakers struggle to reach deal to avert government shutdown
item.description: Congressional negotiators are struggling to reach a deal to keep the government's lights on past Dec. 20. Text of a continuing resolution (CR) for a government funding package was expected Sunday ahead of the looming shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Former longtime South Carolina congressman John Spratt dies at 82
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — John Spratt, a former longtime Democratic congressman from South Carolina who successfully pushed for a balanced budget deal in the 1990s but was unseated decades later when his district turned Republican, has died. He was 82.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and MEG KINNARD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jerry Brown on California issues, the law-making process, and the challenges of governing
item.description: Former California Governor Jerry Brown discussed the state of California's economy, the law-making process, the outcome of the presidential election, and Governor Gavin Newsom's call for a special legislative session to "Trump-proof" California.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: ABC agrees to give $15 million to Trump's presidential library to settle lawsuit
item.description: ABC News agreed to pay $15 million to Trump's presidential library to settle a lawsuit over George Stephanopoulos' inaccurate on-air assertion that Trump was found liable for raping E. Jean Carroll.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Biden urges Congress to 'do something' for gun reform on Sandy Hook anniversary
item.description: Biden in a press release touted his administration's efforts to combat gun violence but argued that there was more to be done — especially as his presidency enters the final stretch.
item.dc:creator: Steff Danielle Thomas, NewsNation

item.title: Trump, close allies attend Army-Navy rivalry game
item.description: As national concerns about the drones spotted on the East Coast continue to mount, President-elect Trump is attending the Army-Navy game.
item.dc:creator: The Hill Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Pelosi has hip replacement surgery at a US military hospital in Germany after a fall
item.description: Pelosi, 84, “is well on the mend,” said Ian Krager, a spokesman for the California Democrat, in a statement.
item.dc:creator: MARY CLARE JALONICK and LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: 5 ways congressional investigations could change in the next Congress and beyond
item.description: The future of congressional investigations could be defined by Republican eagerness to probe private industry and less willingness to defer to the Justice Department, experts say.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: ACLU: Officials should enact law 'firewall' ahead of Trump 2nd term
item.description: ACLU leaders told press Wednesday that state and city governments should prepare for the implementation of ideas from Project 2025.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Mayor Adams seeks to deport migrants who commit crimes
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams told President-elect Donald Trump’s border czar he would work with him to deport migrants who commit violent crimes in New York City. Adams met with Tom Homan at Gracie Mansion to discuss Trump’s plans for mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: NYC Council speaker concerned about deportation plans
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) -- New York City Council Speaker Adrienne Adams has concerns about Mayor Eric Adams' goal to deport migrants who commit violent crimes in the city. Mayor Adams met with incoming border czar Tom Homan on Thursday to discuss President-elect Donald Trump’s plans for mass deportations.
item.dc:creator: Dan Mannarino, PIX 11

item.title: Iran has new drone ship in Persian Gulf
item.description: Iran has a new ship with the capability of launching drones, according to satellite images reviewed by Maxar Technologies that were shared with The Hill. The new drone ship, called Shahid Bagheri, is anchored offshore of the Iranian naval port of Bandar Abbas in the Persian Gulf, according to Maxar.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, NewsNation

item.title: Nancy Pelosi hospitalized after she 'sustained an injury' on official trip to Luxembourg
item.description: Former House Speaker Nancy Pelosi has been hospitalized after she “sustained an injury” during an official engagement in Luxembourg, according to a spokesman.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Gov. Tim Walz says he’ll propose anti-fraud measures, reflects on VP bid
item.description: In his first interview with MPR News since his vice presidential run, Tim Walz said he still has “privilege” of serving as Minnesota governor, urged lawmakers to work together in divided government.
item.dc:creator: Dana Ferguson, MPR News

item.title: Two lawmakers race to expand gun laws
item.description: Concerns about gun violence in St. Pete come as State Representative Dr. Joel Rudman and State Senator Randy Fine juggle gun laws in Tallahassee amid running for Congressional districts 1 and 6.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump trolls Trudeau as 'governor' of Canada
item.description: President-elect Trump mocked Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau, calling him the "governor" of the "Great State of Canada.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, Fox 5

item.title: Congress races toward fast-approaching government shutdown deadline
item.description: Congress has just 10 days until government funding is set to run out, and lawmakers don’t have a deal to keep the lights on during the holidays. Members on both sides of the aisle expect the government will stay open past the Dec. 20 shutdown deadline.
item.dc:creator: Aris Folley, Fox 5
